title: Live collaboration explicitly excludes cursors, CRDTs, typing indicators, and server-side relays
content:
## Explicit non-goals

The design lists what live collaboration will *not* include, to prevent scope creep and clarify the minimum viable loop:

- **No cursor tracking, caret overlays, or per-editor presence.** Custom editors (canvases, tables, button grids) make a generic cursor notion meaningless. It would need per-editor implementation and was a misread of the user's actual goal.
- **No typing indicators.** Already scratched at the chat level for the same reason — latency beats the signal, so the indicator is wrong more often than it's right.
- **No custom CRDTs or merge logic.** The reactor's operation model is the conflict resolver. Adding a CRDT layer on top would duplicate responsibility.
- **No server-side relay.** Fully peer-to-peer via Bee nodes. No central aggregator, no sync service.
- **No chat-level presence dots.**
- **No "save point" or "version" layer beyond the existing keyframe/operation model.**

Non-goals restated at the end of the doc as a guardrail. The single live surface is the toolbar History view, which updates with each peer's ops attributed by signer address.
description: Explicit non-goals: no cursor tracking, no CRDTs, no typing indicators, no server-side relay, no save-point layer beyond the reactor's existing operation model.
